meta.name: STAI-6 State Anxiety Inventory Short Form
meta.description: The STAI-6 is a 6-item short form of the Spielberger State-Trait Anxiety Inventory (STAI) state scale. It provides a rapid and reliable measure of state anxiety, suitable for repeated assessments and time-limited settings while maintaining good psychometric properties.
meta.author: Marteau & Bekker (1992)
meta.reference: Marteau TM, Bekker H. The development of a six-item short-form of the state scale of the Spielberger State-Trait Anxiety Inventory (STAI). Br J Clin Psychol. 1992;31(3):301-306.
meta.instruction: A number of statements which people have used to describe themselves are given below. Read each statement and then select the most appropriate response to indicate how you feel right now, at this moment.

ranges.label: Low Anxiety
ranges.description: Your state anxiety level is low. You appear to be feeling calm and relaxed at this moment.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current state of calm
Practice regular relaxation techniques to sustain well-being
Note what strategies help you feel at ease for future reference

ranges.label: Mild Anxiety
ranges.description: You are experiencing mild state anxiety. This is a common level that most people experience in everyday situations.
suggestions: Try deep breathing or progressive muscle relaxation
Identify any immediate stressors and address them if possible
Engage in a brief mindfulness exercise

ranges.label: Moderate Anxiety
ranges.description: Your current anxiety level is moderate. You may benefit from using coping strategies to manage your state of tension.
suggestions: Take a break from the situation causing anxiety if possible
Consider talking to someone about what you are feeling

ranges.label: High Anxiety
ranges.description: Your state anxiety level is high. You are experiencing significant tension and worry at this moment, which may impair your functioning.
suggestions: Seek immediate support from a trusted person or professional
Practice calming strategies such as slow, deep breathing
Remove yourself from overwhelming situations if safe to do so
Consider consulting a mental health professional if this level persists
